# SRC014 - Online Advertising Incrementality Testing Tutorial

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Measurement |
| Authors / source | Barajas et al. |
| Year | 2021 |
| Source type | Tutorial / research synthesis |
| Quality | Medium-High |
| URL | [https://joel-barajas.github.io/kdd2021-incrementality-testing/tutorial_detailed.pdf](https://joel-barajas.github.io/kdd2021-incrementality-testing/tutorial_detailed.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Tutorial; not a single empirical study.

## Core takeaway

Incrementality testing requires experiment design, not just dashboard interpretation.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to structure holdout, geo, ghost-ad, and audience-split designs.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC014
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
